skill.name: firecrawl-crawl
skill.description: Bulk extract content from a website or site section with the Firecrawl crawl command. It supports scoped paths, crawl limits, depth limits, progress waiting, and output files.
skill.summary: Crawl website sections with Firecrawl and save bulk page extraction results.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: The skill intentionally grants Bash access to Firecrawl CLI commands that crawl user supplied URLs and write results to local files. The static weak cryptography, Ruby backtick, and path traversal alerts are false positives in Markdown descriptions and links, but the intended network crawling and output file behavior warrant a medium risk warning. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential exfiltration, hidden scripts, or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution for Website Crawling
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill allows Bash execution of firecrawl and npx firecrawl commands, then shows commands that crawl user supplied URLs. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can access external sites and should be used only with authorized targets.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools block explicitly permits Firecrawl CLI execution, and the quick start shows crawl commands. The behavior is intentional and documented, with no evidence of hidden command construction.

medium_findings.title: Network Crawling Can Reach User Supplied Targets
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The examples crawl a placeholder URL and describe bulk extraction from many pages. This creates normal web crawling risk, including accidental crawling of unauthorized, private, or high cost targets.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill purpose and command examples clearly require network crawling. The risk depends on user target selection, so this is elevated but not malicious.

medium_findings.title: User Controlled Output File Path
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The documented commands write crawl results with -o, and the options table exposes --output <path>. A careless output path could overwrite local files or place crawl data in an unintended location.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The output path option is explicitly documented, but there is no wrapper code that expands or modifies paths. The practical risk is from user choice rather than malicious implementation.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static Ruby or shell backtick alerts are Markdown inline code, command examples, or option names. They are documentation text, not Ruby execution syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside Markdown bullets, fenced code blocks, and a Markdown table. SKILL.md contains no Ruby source or dynamic shell interpolation.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Misclassified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The ../ sequences are Markdown links to nearby skill documentation. They are not used as runtime file paths and do not read or write the filesystem.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The entire evidence is in the See also section as links to other SKILL.md files. There is no command or code path that consumes these links.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines contain front matter description text and a Markdown table header. No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or cryptographic API appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic operation at the reported locations or elsewhere in SKILL.md. The static detector matched unrelated prose or formatting.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Allowlist for Firecrawl Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill permits Bash(firecrawl *) and Bash(npx firecrawl *). This is a powerful pattern because it runs an external CLI that performs network activity.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration is direct evidence of external command permission. The command scope is limited to Firecrawl, which reduces but does not remove risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Crawl Output Written to Local Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples and options document local output paths for crawl results. Users should keep output under a project-local directory and avoid sensitive destinations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file write behavior is documented through -o and --output. No evidence shows forced unsafe paths, so the concern is contextual.

content.value_statement: Large sites are hard to collect page by page. This skill runs Firecrawl crawl commands with scope, depth, limit, and output controls.
actual_capabilities: Runs Firecrawl crawl commands for a website or site section.
Limits crawls by included paths, excluded paths, page count, and depth.
Waits for crawl completion or checks an existing crawl job.
Controls crawl delay, concurrency, progress display, and pretty output.
Writes crawl results to a selected local output file.
limitations: Requires Firecrawl CLI access or npx Firecrawl availability.
Crawls consume Firecrawl credits for each processed page.
Only reaches pages that Firecrawl can access from the web.
Does not summarize or transform extracted content by itself.

use_cases.title: Collect Documentation Pages
use_cases.description: Crawl every page under a documentation path and save the results for review or analysis.

use_cases.title: Build Research Corpora
use_cases.description: Extract many related pages from one site section for comparison, search, or content analysis.

use_cases.title: Audit Site Coverage
use_cases.description: Use limits and depth settings to inspect which pages are available under a target section.

prompt_templates.title: Crawl a Documentation Section
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner crawl with a clear path limit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Crawl the documentation section at <url> under /docs. Limit it to 50 pages and save the results in .firecrawl/crawl.json.

prompt_templates.title: Crawl with Depth and Progress
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate crawl where completion status matters.
prompt_templates.prompt: Crawl <url> with a maximum depth of 3. Wait for completion, show progress, and keep the output in the project .firecrawl folder.

prompt_templates.title: Scope a Large Site Safely
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate crawl that avoids unwanted paths.
prompt_templates.prompt: Crawl <url> only under /guides and /reference. Exclude /blog, limit the crawl to 100 pages, and use a conservative request delay.

prompt_templates.title: Resume or Check a Crawl Job
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with an existing crawl job.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the status of crawl job <job-id>. If it is complete, explain where the results are stored and summarize the crawl settings used.

output_examples.input: Crawl a docs section with a 50 page limit.
output_examples.output: A completed crawl saved to a project-local output file, with pages restricted to the requested documentation path.

output_examples.input: Run a depth-limited crawl and wait for completion.
output_examples.output: A progress-aware crawl that stops at the selected depth and returns results when Firecrawl finishes processing.
A status update for the crawl job, including whether results are ready or more waiting is needed.

best_practices: Crawl only domains and paths you are authorized to access.
Use include paths, limits, and depth settings before crawling large sites.
Write output to a project-local folder such as .firecrawl.
anti_patterns: Do not crawl an entire site when one section is enough.
Do not use high concurrency against sites with strict rate limits.
Do not write output files over source files or sensitive local paths.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps run Firecrawl crawl commands to extract many pages from a website or site section.

faq.question: Does it require Firecrawl?
faq.answer: Yes. It expects the Firecrawl CLI or npx Firecrawl command to be available.

faq.question: Can it limit the crawl scope?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents include paths, exclude paths, page limits, depth limits, delay, and concurrency options.

faq.question: Where are results saved?
faq.answer: The examples save results under .firecrawl/crawl.json, and the output path can be changed.

faq.question: Is it safe for private sites?
faq.answer: Use it only for sites and paths you are authorized to crawl. Review credentials and access rules before running it.

faq.question: Does it summarize crawled pages?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on crawling and extraction. Summaries require a separate analysis step.
